content:
# Buy a Cheap Peugeot 107 UK: Your Honest Buyer's Guide

Alright, mate? Looking for a car that won't break the bank, is a doddle to drive, and sips fuel like a vicar at a wine tasting? You're probably looking for a **cheap Peugeot 107 UK**! This plucky little city car, along with its siblings the Citroën C1 and Toyota Aygo, has been a hugely popular choice for years, especially for first-time buyers, students, or anyone needing a reliable, no-nonsense runaround.

Here at BuyAndScrap, we know a thing or two about getting a great deal on a used car. We've put together this comprehensive guide to help you understand why the 107 is such a good shout, what to look out for, and how to bag yourself a fantastic example without any nasty surprises.

## Why the Peugeot 107 is a Cracking Little Car

Before we dive into the nitty-gritty of potential issues, let's celebrate why the 107 makes such a strong case for itself as a used car bargain.

### Economical to Run
This is arguably the 107's biggest selling point. Powered predominantly by a tiny 1.0-litre petrol engine, it's incredibly frugal, often achieving 60+ miles per gallon. Insurance groups are typically low (3-6), making it ideal for younger drivers, and many earlier models even boast zero road tax – a massive bonus in the current climate.

### City Slicker by Design
Measuring just 3.4 metres long, the 107 is ridiculously easy to park and manoeuvre in tight urban spaces. Its turning circle is impressively small, allowing you to U-turn in places other cars wouldn't dare. If your daily commute involves navigating busy streets or you just need a car for the school run and local shops, the 107 is perfectly at home.

### Surprisingly Reliable (for the Price)
Thanks to its Toyota Aygo platform and engine, the 107 benefits from Japanese engineering know-how. This means it's generally a very reliable car. Its simple mechanics mean there's less to go wrong, and parts are readily available and affordable when they do.

## What to Check When Buying a Cheap Peugeot 107 UK

Even the best budget cars have their quirks. Here’s what to keep an eye out for when you're searching for a **cheap Peugeot 107 UK** to ensure you bag a good one.

### The Engine – Generally Solid, But Listen Up
Most 107s come with the 1.0-litre three-cylinder petrol engine. These are chain-driven, so no cambelt changes to worry about! Listen for any excessive rattling from the timing chain, especially on start-up. Check for unusual smoke from the exhaust (blue means oil, white means coolant) and ensure the idle is smooth. Minor oil leaks aren't uncommon on older cars but shouldn't be excessive.

### Gearbox & Clutch – The Usual Suspects

#### Manual Gearbox
The manual gearbox is generally robust, but the clutch can be a wear point, particularly if the car has spent its life in stop-start city traffic. Test for a high biting point or slip when accelerating hard in a higher gear – these are signs the clutch is on its way out. A new clutch can be a significant expense on a cheap car.

#### Automated Manual (2-Tronic/M-MT)
Some 107s come with an automated manual transmission. These can be a bit jerky and slow to change gears, which is often mistaken for a fault – it's just how they are. Ensure it engages gears smoothly (for an AMT) and doesn't clunk excessively. Test it on an incline to check if it holds without rolling back too much.

### Suspension & Steering – Bumps and Grinds
Listen for knocking noises over bumps, which could indicate worn anti-roll bar links or suspension bushes. The top mounts on the front suspension can also wear, leading to clonking. While not a huge issue, they're worth factoring into your budget. Check for any excessive play in the steering.

### Electrical Gremlins – Annoying, Not Always Serious
The electric windows can be slow, sometimes seize up, or the motor might fail. Central locking can occasionally play up. Check all dashboard warning lights come on and go out as they should. Another common, albeit minor, issue is the heater fan resistor failing, meaning only one fan speed works (usually full blast).

### Bodywork & Interior – Scratches and Scuffs
As city cars, expect some minor dings, scratches, and stone chips. Check for any more significant accident damage, inconsistent panel gaps, or overspray. While generally good at resisting rust, give the sills, wheel arches, and underneath a visual inspection, especially on older models. The interior plastics are pretty basic and durable, but check for excessive wear on seats and controls.

## Top Tips for Nailing Your Cheap Peugeot 107 UK Purchase

Beyond the car itself, a bit of savvy buying can save you a headache and ensure you get a truly cracking **cheap Peugeot 107 UK**.

### The Test Drive is Key
Don't rush it. Drive the car from cold and hot. Test all gears, brakes, and steering. Listen for every rattle, squeak, and clunk. Ensure the car tracks straight and doesn't pull to one side.

### Check the Paperwork
Always ask for a full-service history (even if it's just a few stamps) and old MOT certificates. Verify the V5C (logbook) matches the seller's address and the VIN on the car. Don't be afraid to ask questions!

### HPI Check & MOT History
These are non-negotiable. An HPI check will tell you if the car has been written off, has outstanding finance, or has been stolen. The free online MOT history checker (found on gov.uk) is an absolute goldmine for spotting recurring advisories that might point to neglected issues. Read our full [used car buying guide](/used-car-buying-guide) for more tips on what to look for.

### Consider a Professional Inspection
If you're unsure or not mechanically minded, consider paying a mobile mechanic to give the car a pre-purchase inspection. It’s a small investment for massive peace of mind.

## Is a Peugeot 107 the Right Car for You?

If you're looking for an affordable, economical, and surprisingly fun-to-drive small car for city life, commuting, or as a reliable first car, then a Peugeot 107 is an excellent choice. It’s not built for long motorway journeys or hauling massive amounts of luggage, but for its intended purpose, it's brilliant. And if you're looking to upgrade, remember you can always [sell your current car](/selling-your-car) with us too!

## Find Your Next Cheap Peugeot 107 UK Today!

The Peugeot 107 truly represents fantastic value as a compact, economical, and generally reliable little runner. It’s perfect for getting you from A to B without fuss, and without emptying your wallet. If you’re in the market for a **cheap Peugeot 107 UK**, you've come to the right place. Ready to find your perfect little city car?
